title: Pockets and envelopes for journal pages
sources: SYNTHESISED — general journalling-craft practice; no single PD canonical source
notes: Three pocket types: cut-and-glue inside pocket (one sheet scored and folded, glued to the page on three sides), the simple envelope fold (diagonal corners brought in, bottom folded up), and the full origami-style envelope from a single sheet. Which works for which use: storing pressed flowers, folded notes, receipts, ticket stubs. Keeps scope on the physical making.
